title: Convictions, Somewhat
description: Defend your neon convictions by tapping true-belief orbs on sight and timing the somewhat-belief orbs only at the last safe moment.

howToPlayJson.title: Convictions, Somewhat
howToPlayJson.objective: Last through a 3-minute neon belief rush, protect your 3 Certainty hearts, and post the highest offline leaderboard score by judging which moving orbs deserve instant conviction and which only deserve somewhat commitment.

controls.outcome: Scores points, extends combo, and plays a sharp synth chime

controls.input: Tap a dashed-ring orb only when its inner ring shrinks into the highlighted hit
controls.outcome: Grants a bonus burst, powers your multiplier, and plays a softer delayed tone

controls.input: Do not tap cracked red orbs
controls.outcome: Prevents losing Certainty hearts and combo

steps: Neon orbs drift in from screen edges with large color-coded rings that clearly show their rule: solid means tap now, dashed means wait for a
At the top of the screen, two giant badges show the current active beliefs: one Conviction color and one Somewhat color.
Every 6 seconds, a siren triggers a 'Somewhat Shift' and one badge swaps state, forcing you to immediately change from fast tapping to late,
Missing a solid Conviction orb or tapping a red Doubt orb removes 1 Certainty heart; lose all 3 and the run ends.
Correct hits build a combo meter; every 10 clean judgments raises the score multiplier and adds another music layer.
Orb speed, spawn count, and state swaps ramp up over the session so the final minute becomes a readable but demanding judgment scramble.
howToPlayJson.scoring: Solid Conviction taps are worth base points, perfect Somewhat judgments are worth bonus points and charge a multiplier, and mistakes reset combo. Each run saves a local offline high-score table built around total score,
tips: Read the ring before the color; the outline tells you the rule faster than the fill.
Use Somewhat orbs to fuel multiplier bursts, not as filler taps.
When the shift siren sounds, glance at the top badges first; that swap is the whole decision pivot.
Protect combo in the first minute so the late-game speed spike pays out bigger points.
